rulings.Case Title: JOE PEARSON ET AL VS. SOCO WEST, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Selena Pearson As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff; For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; And To Add Previously Sued Doe Defendant To The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Substituting Selena Pearson as Successor-in-Interest to, and Substituting Successor-in-Interest for Deceased Plaintiff; For Leave to File First Amended Complaint; and to Add Previously Sued Doe Defendant to the First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. (C.C.P. Sections 377.20, 377.31, 377.11, 377.32, 377.60, and 473.) No opposition filed. Defendant WESTERN ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT TRUST (previously sued as Does 2 and 305) is added as a party to the survival and wrongful death causes of action. Plaintiffs shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint forthwith on the newly added defendant, as well as on those defendants who have not yet appeared in this case. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served on defendants 3M Company and Metropolitan Life Insurance Company only, as they have already appeared in the action and were served with the instant motion. It is further ordered that legal heirs (i) HOLLYETTE LEXINGTON; (ii) TRACY ROEBUCK; and (iii) EDDIE RICHARDSON shall be added as plaintiffs to this action. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 2, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: DALLAS UDOVCH VS. WESTBURNE SUPPLY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(e). 1. The trial date is July 22, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is July 30, 2024. b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 16, 2024. a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation. c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is July 5, 2024. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is July 19, 2024. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 2, 2024. a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. 8. No later than April 16, 2024: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: DALLAS UDOVCH VS. WESTBURNE SUPPLY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF DALLAS UDOVCH MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY)
rulings.Rulings: (Part 2 of 2) (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry) 9. A further case management conference is set for June 4, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. a. Case management conference statements are not required. b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom. c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing. 10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than June 10, 2024. Response to the settlement demand is due June 24, 2024. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on July 1, 2024. 11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, July 18, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 2, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA PERKINS VS. BARRETSS MINERALS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2024, in Department 301, Line 3. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Trial-Setting Preference Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 36 is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(d). Conditional non-opposition filed. 1. The trial date is July 22, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is July 30, 2024. b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 16, 2024. a. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation. c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is July 5, 2024. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is July 19, 2024. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 2, 2024. a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. 8. No later than April 16, 2024: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES E. SAUNDERS II VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 2, 2024, in Department 301, Line 4. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is DENIED without prejudice. No POS of summons and complaint on file for Foster Wheeler LLC. This is matter was continued from the March 26, 2024 calendar to give Plaintiff the opportunity to file proofs of service for several defendants. Despite a continued hearing date, Plaintiff failed to file a proof of service of summons for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC. Contrary to counsel's assertions at the hearing, Foster Wheeler LLC has not appeared in this case. While a party may support their motion for preference with a declaration stating that all essential parties have been served with process or have appeared, (C.C.P. Section 36(c)(1)), any such declaration is undermined where, as here, there is no supporting proof of service or answer on file. A further continuance will not be granted and Plaintiff may re-file his motion when the defect is corrected. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 2, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)
